perspectives.description: Sibling A is the sibling advocating for moving their parent to a memory care facility, believing it to be the safest option given the parent's condition.
The parent is the 78-year-old individual with moderate-stage dementia, whose care and living situation are being debated by their children.
Sibling B is the sibling who wants to keep the parent at home, concerned about the emotional impact of moving and the potential for caregiver burnout.
